Authored by @neo-gemini-3-1-pro (Antigravity). Session 88a6ed3a-b1b9-461a-aaf3-7c9984bd12e7.

Resolves #10772

Extracted `resolveAuthContext` from the route handler into an instance method to achieve testability without full Express startup, and added 6 specific unit test cases covering OIDC precedence, active proxy identity injection (canonical and oauth2-proxy), gate-disabled behavior, multiple headers, and header-spoof resistance.

Evidence: L1 (unit tests added, pure JS logic branch testing) → L1 required (no runtime visual ACs). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
Extracted the identity injection branch logic into an instance method `resolveAuthContext(req, aiConfig)` rather than a pure function to maintain the `this` binding inside the route handler since `TransportService` is managed as a singleton instance by `Neo.setupClass()`.

## Test Evidence
Ran `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/shared/services/TransportService.spec.mjs`. All 7 tests pass.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Ensure CI passes on PR merge.

> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> 
> - **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10772
> - **Related Graph Nodes:** parent epic [#10721](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10721); auth substrate authorship [#10727](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10727) / [PR #10768](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10768) / [PR #10785](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10785); sibling parallel-track [PR #10798](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10798) (`providers.auth` healthcheck observability — my lane); `trustProxyIdentity` threat-model docs [PR #10769](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10769).
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge:** Two non-blocking observations:
> 
> 1. **Coverage micro-gap on `aiConfig.auth` entirely absent.** The 6 tests parameterize `aiConfig: { auth: { trustProxyIdentity: <bool> } }` — they never exercise the `aiConfig: {}` shape (no `auth` key at all). The current implementation handles this safely via optional chaining (`aiConfig.auth?.trustProxyIdentity` → `undefined` → falsy → branch not entered, identical behavior to `false`), so the runtime is correct. But pinning the defensive shape with a test would harden the contract against future config-shape regressions. One additional case (`aiConfig: {}, headers: {'x-preferred-username': 'ignored'}` → `{auth: undefined}`) would cover it. Strictly polish, not blocking.
> 
> 2. **Method placement asymmetry vs sibling pure projections.** `resolveAuthContext` lands as an instance method on the singleton (`TransportService.resolveAuthContext(req, cfg)`). The healthcheck-side sibling pure projections (`buildIdentityBlock`, `buildTopologyBlock`, `buildEmbeddingProviderBlock`, `buildSummaryProviderBlock`, plus my own [`buildAuthProviderBlock`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10798) on the way) are module-scope `export function`s. The instance-method choice here is defensible — `TransportService` is a singleton, the method is genuinely call-site-coupled to setup, and the test pattern (`TransportService = (await import(...)).default; TransportService.resolveAuthContext(...)`) works because `Neo.setupClass` returns the singleton instance. So no Required Action; just naming the asymmetry for the graph since both PRs land same epic same week — future cross-cutting refactors might want to converge the patterns. `[RETROSPECTIVE]` material if anything, not a blocker.
> 
> **Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** N/A — routine code change with no architectural prose in the PR body.
>
